sentenceFrame: Chick has the new blue glue.
celebration: A LIGHT BUNDLE of four small teams — ew, ue, au, aw — the last odd cousins, gathered onto one leaf. The heart word BECAUSE joins the wall: the word that finally lets a child say WHY.

songs.title: New-Blue-True!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(oo… aw… oo… aw…)
Four little teams!

[Hook]
New! Blue! True!
ew and ue — say OO!
New-blue-glue! New-blue-glue!
It's new! (brand new!)

[Verse 2]
Chew! Grew! Flew! Drew!
A clue! A clue!
Say it long — oo, oo, oo!
(new! blue! true!)

[Verse 3]
Saw! Paw! Claw! Draw!
au and aw — say AW!
A hawk on the straw!
(saw! saw! saw!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not new!
(oo… aw… oo… aw…)

[Outro]
ew — new… aw — saw… (oo… aw…)
Brand new! (a clue!)
(oo… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: TWO sounds bundled: ew/ue say long-oo /uː/ (new, blue, chew, true) — hook 1+2; au/aw say /ɔː/ (saw, paw, claw, draw) — verse 3. Keep them cleanly separated so children hear which pair does what. Sung as pure vowels, never letter names. A light week — keep it brisk and playful. Potato bridge intact.

songs.title: The New Blue Glue
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The new blue glue!
The new blue glue!
Where is the glue? (where? where?)
On the claw!

[Verse 2]
Chick has the new blue glue.
Chick can draw!
Chick drew a paw. (a paw!)
Chick saw the glue…

[Verse 3]
The glue is on the claw!
Chick is stuck — because the glue is new!
(stuck! stuck! stuck!)
Chick blew on the glue.

[Outro]
The glue… the claw… (shhh…)
What is in the glue?!
(POTATO!)
The new blue glue. (all stuck up!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The new blue glue.' — Chick tries to draw and gets gloriously stuck. Introduces the heart word BECAUSE (sung whole) — the word that lets a child give a reason for the first time. Keep it silly and sticky; the 'stuck!' chant is the fun. Whisper the outro; the potato is glued in too.

spreads.artNote: A bottle of bright blue glue in the spotlight, a fresh drip forming at the nozzle. Shiny and new.

spreads.text: Chick has the new blue glue.
spreads.artNote: Chick — the tiny fluffy yellow chick with a stubby orange beak — cradling the big blue glue bottle, thrilled.
Chick with a crayon in its beak, mid-scribble on a big sheet, tongue-out concentration.
Chick proudly beside a crayon drawing of a big animal paw. 'Drew' — the past of draw, ew again.
Chick's eyes going wide as the blue glue tips over, a slow blue river creeping toward the drawing.

spreads.text: The glue is on the claw!
spreads.artNote: The drawn paw's claw now smeared with real blue glue; Chick's own foot stuck in the puddle. Oh no.

spreads.text: Chick is stuck because the glue is new!
spreads.artNote: Chick comically fixed to the spot, one foot glued down, tugging. The heart word BECAUSE printed — a reason given. Snake peeks in, amused.
Chick blowing hard on the glue to dry it, cheeks puffed, feathers ruffling. 'Blew' — ew once more.
Set into the drying blue glue like a footprint in wet cement: a single potato, half-sunk. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Chick and Snake stare at it, stuck.

sentences: Chick has the new blue glue.
Chick is stuck because the glue is new.
extras: TWO-FAMILY SORT: this leaf holds four small teams doing two jobs. ew + ue say long-oo (new, blue, chew, true, clue). au + aw say /ɔː/ (saw, paw, cause, straw). Two hoops, sort by sound — the tidy end of the vowel teams before the y-vowel and the ghost letters.

HEART WORD because: it holds the au team but says its own way — too useful to wait. The moment because joins the wall is the moment a child can answer 'why?' with a reason. Pair it with the why-question game (why did the chick get stuck? BECAUSE…).
Pattern Tree: the class reads new/blue/saw/draw aloud and hangs the ew/ue/au/aw leaf — one leaf, four small cousins.
Moveable alphabet: build n-ew, bl-ue, gl-ue, s-aw, dr-aw, cl-aw, str-aw.

teacherFocus: A light bundle to close the vowel teams — four small cousins on one leaf. Keep the two jobs clear: ew and ue say long-oo (new, blue, chew, clue); au and aw say /ɔː/ (saw, paw, straw, cause). Two hoops, sort by sound, don't over-drill — this is a breath before the y-vowel. The real gift of the week is the heart word BECAUSE: the first time a child can attach a reason to a thought. Play the why→because game (why did Chick get stuck? Because the glue was new!) — it lands right before why itself earns its letters next week.
